skill.name: convertkit-automation
skill.description: Automate Kit email marketing tasks through Rube MCP, including subscriber lookup, tagging, unsubscribes, broadcasts, and broadcast statistics.
skill.summary: Manage Kit subscribers, tags, broadcasts, and stats through Rube MCP workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans and fenced examples that name MCP tools and parameters. No executable scripts, shell commands, weak cryptography, reconnaissance behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is medium because the skill uses an external MCP endpoint and guides authenticated operations that can unsubscribe subscribers or delete broadcasts.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Kit Operations Can Modify Audience Data
medium_findings.description: The skill guides actions that tag subscribers, unsubscribe subscribers, and delete broadcasts. These are legitimate Kit workflows, but they can change account state and should require explicit user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced workflows explicitly include subscriber tagging, subscriber deletion, and broadcast deletion. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the actions match the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Processes Kit Account Data
medium_findings.description: The skill requires users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and connect a Kit account. Subscriber and broadcast operations may pass account data through that external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prerequisites and setup section clearly require Rube MCP and Kit authentication. This confirms external network dependency, but not malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans and fenced examples. They identify MCP tool names, parameters, and example workflows, not executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the backticks are Markdown formatting around tool names and examples. SKILL.md contains documentation only and no Ruby or shell execution context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Rube MCP URL Is Expected Configuration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the documented Rube MCP server used by the skill. This is a network dependency, but it is not hidden or unrelated to the stated function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in setup instructions and matches the declared Rube MCP requirement. It should be disclosed, but it is not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm or system reconnaissance behavior was found. The flagged lines contain product names, parameter names, or quick reference table entries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, host probing, port scanning, or environment inspection. The static matches are semantic false positives in documentation text.

content.user_title: Automate Kit Subscriber and Broadcast Tasks
content.value_statement: Kit account work can become repetitive when teams manage subscribers, tags, and broadcasts by hand. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for common Kit automation tasks through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Search and list Kit subscribers with filters, sorting, and cursor pagination.
Find subscriber IDs by exact email address before taking account actions.
Apply existing Kit tags to subscribers for segmentation workflows.
Unsubscribe subscribers after confirming the target subscriber ID.
List broadcasts, inspect broadcast details, and retrieve sent broadcast statistics.
Delete broadcasts after verifying the target broadcast ID.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active authenticated Kit connection.
Does not create new Kit tags because tags must be created in the Kit web UI.
Exact email matching is required for subscriber lookup.
Broadcast statistics are available only for sent broadcasts.

use_cases.title: Segment New Leads
use_cases.description: Find subscribers by email and apply existing tags for campaign segmentation.

use_cases.title: Handle Unsubscribe Requests
use_cases.description: Look up a subscriber, confirm the account record, and unsubscribe the person from all email communications.

use_cases.title: Review Broadcast Performance
use_cases.description: List recent broadcasts, open a selected broadcast, and collect available performance statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Subscriber
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Kit workflow to find the subscriber with this exact email address: [email]. Show the subscriber status and ID.

prompt_templates.title: Tag a Subscriber
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the subscriber for [email], confirm the subscriber ID, then apply existing Kit tag ID [tag_id].

prompt_templates.title: Audit Broadcast Stats
prompt_templates.prompt: List recent Kit broadcasts, help me choose one, then retrieve details and available stats for the selected broadcast.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Safe Cleanup Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Find broadcast ID [broadcast_id], verify its title and status, ask for confirmation, then delete it only after I approve.

output_examples.input: Find a subscriber by email and show the next safe action.
output_examples.output: Subscriber found with active status. The next safe action is to confirm the subscriber ID before tagging or unsubscribing.
Recent sent broadcasts are listed with available statistics. Draft broadcasts are identified as not eligible for performance statistics.
The selected broadcast is verified by ID and title. Deletion is paused until the user gives explicit approval.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so schemas match the current Kit connector.
Confirm subscriber or broadcast IDs before account-changing actions.
Pace bulk operations and handle Kit rate limits with backoff.
anti_patterns: Do not delete broadcasts without verifying the title and ID.
Do not assume partial email search is supported.
Do not treat total count flags as booleans when the workflow expects strings.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Kit API key?
faq.answer: No direct API key is described. It requires Rube MCP and an authenticated Kit connection through Rube.

faq.question: Can it create new Kit tags?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that tags are created in the Kit web UI before assignment.

faq.question: Can it search subscribers by partial email?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses exact email address matching.

faq.question: Can it unsubscribe a subscriber?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide an unsubscribe workflow after finding the subscriber ID.

faq.question: Can it retrieve stats for draft broadcasts?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that stats are available only for sent broadcasts.

faq.question: Is this safe for bulk operations?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill recommends pacing bulk operations and handling rate limit responses.
